# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
"""Multi-process pipeline runner.

The runner owns the single serving path. It can start one OS process containing
multiple non-TP stages, multiple OS processes on the same GPU, and the existing
one-process-per-rank TP topology.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import logging
import multiprocessing
import socket
from typing import Any

from sglang_omni.config.placement import (
    StagePlacementPlan,
    resolve_gpu_stage_names,
    resolve_stage_gpu_ids,
)
from sglang_omni.config.runtime import (
    resolve_stage_factory_arg_defaults,
    resolve_stage_static_factory_args,
)
from sglang_omni.config.schema import PipelineConfig, StageConfig
from sglang_omni.config.topology import ProcessTopologyPlan
from sglang_omni.pipeline import Coordinator
from sglang_omni.pipeline.runtime_config import (
    IpcRuntimeDir,
    PipelineRuntimePrep,
    build_comm_config,
    prepare_pipeline_runtime,
)
from sglang_omni.pipeline.stage_workers import (
    StageGroup,
    StageLaunchConfig,
    StageWorkerProcessSpec,
)
from sglang_omni.utils.imports import import_string

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def _build_stage_groups(
    config: PipelineConfig,
    ctx: multiprocessing.context.BaseContext | None = None,
    *,
    stages_cfg: list[StageConfig],
    name_map: dict[str, str],
    endpoints: dict[str, str],
    placement_plan: StagePlacementPlan,
    process_plan: ProcessTopologyPlan,
) -> list[StageGroup]:
    """Build lifecycle groups from prepared endpoints and process topology.

    The caller owns endpoint allocation and IPC runtime-dir lifecycle. This
    helper only converts prepared runtime state into subprocess specs.
    """
    if ctx is None:
        ctx = multiprocessing.get_context("spawn")

    stage_endpoints = {s.name: endpoints[f"stage_{s.name}"] for s in stages_cfg}
    stream_receivers: set[str] = set()
    for scfg in stages_cfg:
        for target in scfg.stream_to:
            stream_receivers.add(target)
    stage_cfg_by_name = {stage.name: stage for stage in stages_cfg}

    nccl_port_counter = _NcclPortAllocator()

    # GPU-resident stages, shared by every stage so the transport router can
    # decide GPU vs host transport per edge from static placement alone. Include the
    # pre-fusion aliases so lookups work whether an edge names a stage by its
    # raw or canonical (fused) name.
    gpu_canonical = resolve_gpu_stage_names(placement_plan)
    gpu_stage_names = set(gpu_canonical)
    for raw_name, canonical_name in name_map.items():
        if canonical_name in gpu_canonical:
            gpu_stage_names.add(raw_name)
    stage_gpu_ids = {
        name: placement.gpu_ids for name, placement in placement_plan.stages.items()
    }
    for raw_name, canonical_name in name_map.items():
        if canonical_name in stage_gpu_ids:
            stage_gpu_ids[raw_name] = stage_gpu_ids[canonical_name]

    single_stage_specs: dict[str, StageLaunchConfig] = {}
    tp_groups: list[StageGroup] = []
    for stage_cfg in stages_cfg:
        tp_size = stage_cfg.tp_size
        gpu_ids = resolve_stage_gpu_ids(placement_plan, stage_cfg)
        nccl_port = nccl_port_counter.allocate() if tp_size > 1 else None

        same_process_targets = _resolve_same_process_targets(
            stage_cfg,
            stage_cfg_by_name,
            name_map,
            process_plan,
        )

        # Avoid importing stage factories in the parent process. The child
        # injects signature-dependent args after importing the factory it must
        # construct anyway.
        base_factory_args = resolve_stage_static_factory_args(stage_cfg, config)

        stage_kwargs = dict(
            stage_name=stage_cfg.name,
            factory=stage_cfg.factory,
            next_stages=stage_cfg.next,
            route_fn=stage_cfg.route_fn,
            is_terminal=stage_cfg.terminal,
            env_defaults=dict(config.env_defaults),
            wait_for=stage_cfg.wait_for,
            wait_for_fn=stage_cfg.wait_for_fn,
            merge_fn=stage_cfg.merge_fn,
            project_payload={
                name_map.get(target, target): dotted_path
                for target, dotted_path in stage_cfg.project_payload.items()
            },
            coordinator_endpoint=endpoints["completion"],
            abort_endpoint=endpoints["abort"],
            stage_endpoints=stage_endpoints,
            stream_targets=list(stage_cfg.stream_to),
            stream_done_to_fn=stage_cfg.stream_done_to_fn,
            gpu_stage_names=gpu_stage_names,
            stage_gpu_ids=stage_gpu_ids,
            same_process_targets=same_process_targets,
            is_stream_receiver=stage_cfg.name in stream_receivers,
            can_accept_stream_before_payload=stage_cfg.can_accept_stream_before_payload,
            disable_direct_cuda_ipc_payload=stage_cfg.disable_direct_cuda_ipc_payload,
            name_map=name_map,
        )
        if tp_size == 1:
            single_stage_specs[stage_cfg.name] = _build_single_stage_spec(
                stage_cfg=stage_cfg,
                config=config,
                gpu_id=gpu_ids[0],
                recv_endpoint=stage_endpoints[stage_cfg.name],
                base_factory_args=base_factory_args,
                stage_kwargs=stage_kwargs,
            )
        else:
            specs = _build_tp_stage_specs(
                ctx=ctx,
                stage_cfg=stage_cfg,
                config=config,
                gpu_ids=gpu_ids,
                nccl_port=nccl_port,
                recv_endpoint=stage_endpoints[stage_cfg.name],
                base_factory_args=base_factory_args,
                stage_kwargs=stage_kwargs,
            )
            process_specs = [
                StageWorkerProcessSpec(
                    process_name=process_plan.tp_stage_to_processes[stage_cfg.name][
                        spec.tp_rank
                    ],
                    stage_specs=[spec],
                )
                for spec in specs
            ]
            tp_groups.append(StageGroup(stage_cfg.name, process_specs))

    groups: list[StageGroup] = []
    for group in process_plan.groups:
        groups.append(
            StageGroup(
                group.name,
                [
                    StageWorkerProcessSpec(
                        process_name=group.name,
                        stage_specs=[
                            single_stage_specs[stage_name]
                            for stage_name in group.stage_names
                        ],
                    )
                ],
            )
        )
    groups.extend(tp_groups)

    return groups


def _resolve_same_process_targets(
    stage_cfg: StageConfig,
    stage_cfg_by_name: dict[str, StageConfig],
    name_map: dict[str, str],
    process_plan: ProcessTopologyPlan,
) -> set[str]:
    if stage_cfg.tp_size > 1:
        return set()
    source_process = process_plan.stage_to_process.get(stage_cfg.name)
    if source_process is None:
        return set()

    raw_targets: list[str] = []
    if stage_cfg.next is not None:
        raw_targets.extend(
            [stage_cfg.next] if isinstance(stage_cfg.next, str) else stage_cfg.next
        )
    raw_targets.extend(stage_cfg.stream_to)

    same_process_targets: set[str] = set()
    for raw_target in raw_targets:
        target = name_map.get(raw_target, raw_target)
        target_cfg = stage_cfg_by_name.get(target)
        if target_cfg is None or target_cfg.tp_size > 1:
            continue
        if process_plan.stage_to_process.get(target) == source_process:
            same_process_targets.add(target)
    return same_process_targets


def _build_single_stage_spec(
    *,
    stage_cfg: StageConfig,
    config: PipelineConfig,
    gpu_id: int | None,
    recv_endpoint: str,
    base_factory_args: dict[str, Any],
    stage_kwargs: dict[str, Any],
) -> StageLaunchConfig:
    factory_args = dict(base_factory_args)
    comm_config = _resolve_comm_config(stage_cfg, gpu_id=gpu_id)
    return StageLaunchConfig(
        role="single",
        tp_rank=0,
        tp_size=1,
        placement_gpu_id=gpu_id,
        gpu_id=gpu_id,
        nccl_port=None,
        factory_args=factory_args,
        factory_arg_defaults=resolve_stage_factory_arg_defaults(
            stage_cfg, config, gpu_id=gpu_id
        ),
        comm_config=comm_config,
        recv_endpoint=recv_endpoint,
        **stage_kwargs,
    )


def _build_tp_stage_specs(
    *,
    ctx: multiprocessing.context.BaseContext,
    stage_cfg: StageConfig,
    config: PipelineConfig,
    gpu_ids: list[int | None],
    nccl_port: int | None,
    recv_endpoint: str,
    base_factory_args: dict[str, Any],
    stage_kwargs: dict[str, Any],
) -> list[StageLaunchConfig]:
    follower_work_queues = [ctx.Queue() for _ in range(stage_cfg.tp_size - 1)]
    follower_abort_queues = [ctx.Queue() for _ in range(stage_cfg.tp_size - 1)]
    follower_admin_result_queues = [ctx.Queue() for _ in range(stage_cfg.tp_size - 1)]
    specs: list[StageLaunchConfig] = []

    for tp_rank in range(stage_cfg.tp_size):
        gpu_id = gpu_ids[tp_rank] if tp_rank < len(gpu_ids) else gpu_ids[0]
        if gpu_id is None:
            raise ValueError(f"TP stage {stage_cfg.name!r} requires GPU placement")
        factory_args = dict(base_factory_args)
        factory_args["tp_rank"] = tp_rank
        factory_args["tp_size"] = stage_cfg.tp_size
        factory_args["nccl_port"] = nccl_port

        comm_config = _resolve_comm_config(stage_cfg, gpu_id=gpu_id)

        if tp_rank == 0:
            specs.append(
                StageLaunchConfig(
                    role="leader",
                    tp_rank=tp_rank,
                    tp_size=stage_cfg.tp_size,
                    placement_gpu_id=gpu_id,
                    gpu_id=gpu_id,
                    nccl_port=nccl_port,
                    factory_args=factory_args,
                    factory_arg_defaults=resolve_stage_factory_arg_defaults(
                        stage_cfg, config, gpu_id=gpu_id
                    ),
                    comm_config=comm_config,
                    recv_endpoint=recv_endpoint,
                    follower_work_queues=follower_work_queues,
                    follower_abort_queues=follower_abort_queues,
                    follower_admin_result_queues=follower_admin_result_queues,
                    **stage_kwargs,
                )
            )
            continue

        idx = tp_rank - 1
        specs.append(
            StageLaunchConfig(
                role="follower",
                tp_rank=tp_rank,
                tp_size=stage_cfg.tp_size,
                placement_gpu_id=gpu_id,
                gpu_id=gpu_id,
                nccl_port=nccl_port,
                factory_args=factory_args,
                factory_arg_defaults=resolve_stage_factory_arg_defaults(
                    stage_cfg, config, gpu_id=gpu_id
                ),
                comm_config=comm_config,
                recv_endpoint="",
                internal_work_queue=follower_work_queues[idx],
                internal_abort_queue=follower_abort_queues[idx],
                internal_admin_result_queue=follower_admin_result_queues[idx],
                **stage_kwargs,
            )
        )

    return specs


def _resolve_comm_config(
    stage_cfg: StageConfig,
    *,
    gpu_id: int | None,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Build stage-local communication options from placement."""
    comm_config = build_comm_config(stage_cfg)
    if stage_cfg.gpu is not None:
        comm_config["gpu_id"] = gpu_id
    return comm_config


class _NcclPortAllocator:
    """Allocate unique NCCL ports for per-stage TP groups."""

    def __init__(self, base_port: int = 29500):
        self._next = base_port

    def allocate(self) -> int:
        """Return an available port, incrementing the counter."""
        while True:
            port = self._next
            self._next += 1
            try:
                with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as s:
                    s.bind(("127.0.0.1", port))
                    return port
            except OSError:
                continue


class MultiProcessPipelineRunner:

    def __init__(self, config: PipelineConfig):
        self._config = config
        self._coordinator: Coordinator | None = None
        self._ipc_runtime_dir: IpcRuntimeDir | None = None
        self._groups: list[StageGroup] = []
        self._completion_task: asyncio.Task | None = None
        self._monitor_task: asyncio.Task | None = None
        self._fatal_event: asyncio.Event | None = None
        self._fatal_error: BaseException | None = None
        self._prep: PipelineRuntimePrep | None = None
        self._started = False

    @property
    def coordinator(self) -> Coordinator:
        if self._coordinator is None:
            raise RuntimeError("Runner not started")
        return self._coordinator

    @property
    def prep(self) -> PipelineRuntimePrep:
        """Return the resolved runtime prep (placement plan, process plan,
        endpoints, fused stages). Valid only after :meth:`start`."""
        if self._prep is None:
            raise RuntimeError("Runner not started")
        return self._prep

    @property
    def stage_control_endpoints(self) -> dict[str, str]:
        if not self._started:
            raise RuntimeError("Runner not started")
        endpoints: dict[str, str] = {}
        for group in self._groups:
            endpoints.update(group.stage_control_endpoints)
        return endpoints

    async def start(self, timeout: float = 120.0) -> None:
        if self._started:
            raise RuntimeError("Already started")

        try:
            ctx = multiprocessing.get_context("spawn")
            self._fatal_event = asyncio.Event()
            self._fatal_error = None
            prep = prepare_pipeline_runtime(
                self._config,
                ipc_runtime_dir=self._ipc_runtime_dir,
            )
            self._prep = prep
            self._ipc_runtime_dir = prep.runtime_dir
            groups = _build_stage_groups(
                self._config,
                ctx,
                stages_cfg=prep.stages_cfg,
                name_map=prep.name_map,
                endpoints=prep.endpoints,
                placement_plan=prep.placement_plan,
                process_plan=prep.process_plan,
            )

            terminal_stages_resolver = (
                import_string(self._config.terminal_stages_fn)
                if self._config.terminal_stages_fn
                else None
            )
            self._coordinator = Coordinator(
                completion_endpoint=prep.endpoints["completion"],
                abort_endpoint=prep.endpoints["abort"],
                entry_stage=prep.entry_stage,
                terminal_stages=self._config.terminal_stages or None,
                terminal_stages_resolver=terminal_stages_resolver,
            )
            await self._coordinator.start()
            self._completion_task = asyncio.create_task(
                self._coordinator.run_completion_loop()
            )

            self._groups = groups
            if self._config.env_defaults:
                env_names = ", ".join(sorted(self._config.env_defaults))
                logger.info(f"Configured stage process env defaults: {env_names}")
            for group in self._groups:
                group.spawn(ctx)

            await asyncio.gather(*(g.wait_ready(timeout) for g in self._groups))

            for group in self._groups:
                if group.any_dead():
                    raise RuntimeError(
                        f"Stage process(es) died during startup: "
                        f"{group.dead_summary()}"
                    )

            for group in self._groups:
                for stage_name, endpoint in group.stage_control_endpoints.items():
                    self._coordinator.register_stage(stage_name, endpoint)

            self._started = True
            self._monitor_task = asyncio.create_task(self._monitor_children())

            total_stages = sum(
                len(group.stage_control_endpoints) for group in self._groups
            )
            total_procs = sum(g.process_count for g in self._groups)
            logger.info(
                "MultiProcessPipelineRunner started: %d stage(s), %d process(es)",
                total_stages,
                total_procs,
            )

        except Exception:
            await self._cleanup_on_failure()
            raise

    async def _monitor_children(self) -> None:
        while self._started:
            for group in self._groups:
                if group.any_dead():
                    error = RuntimeError(
                        f"Dead stage process(es) detected: {group.dead_summary()}"
                    )
                    logger.error("%s", error)
                    await self._fail_runtime(error)
                    return
            await asyncio.sleep(5.0)

    async def _fail_runtime(self, error: BaseException) -> None:
        self._fatal_error = error
        if self._coordinator is not None:
            await self._coordinator.fail_pending_requests(error)
        if self._fatal_event is not None:
            self._fatal_event.set()
        await self.stop()

    async def wait_failed(self) -> None:
        if self._fatal_event is None:
            raise RuntimeError("Runner not started")
        await self._fatal_event.wait()
        if self._fatal_error is not None:
            raise self._fatal_error
        raise RuntimeError("Pipeline runtime failed")

    async def _cancel_completion_task(self) -> None:
        if self._completion_task is None:
            return
        self._completion_task.cancel()
        try:
            await self._completion_task
        except asyncio.CancelledError:
            pass
        self._completion_task = None

    def _close_runtime_dir(self) -> None:
        if self._ipc_runtime_dir is None:
            return
        self._ipc_runtime_dir.close()
        self._ipc_runtime_dir = None

    async def stop(self) -> None:
        if not self._started:
            return
        self._started = False

        if self._monitor_task is not None:
            current = asyncio.current_task()
            if current != self._monitor_task:
                self._monitor_task.cancel()
            self._monitor_task = None

        # Send shutdown to stages via coordinator
        try:
            await self._coordinator.shutdown_stages()
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning("shutdown_stages error: %s", e)

        # Shutdown all groups
        await asyncio.gather(
            *(g.shutdown() for g in self._groups),
            return_exceptions=True,
        )

        await self._cancel_completion_task()

        await self._coordinator.stop()
        self._groups.clear()
        self._coordinator = None

        self._close_runtime_dir()

    async def _cleanup_on_failure(self) -> None:
        """Best-effort cleanup after a failed start()."""
        for group in self._groups:
            for p in group.processes:
                if p.is_alive():
                    p.terminate()
            for p in group.processes:
                p.join(timeout=5)
                if p.is_alive():
                    p.kill()
                    p.join(timeout=2)
            group.close_control_channels()
        self._groups.clear()

        await self._cancel_completion_task()

        if self._coordinator is not None:
            try:
                await self._coordinator.stop()
            except Exception:
                pass
            self._coordinator = None

        self._close_runtime_dir()

35	@dataclass
36	class StageLaunchConfig:
37	    """Resolved launch metadata for one logical stage instance.
38	
39	    ``StageWorkerProcessSpec`` is the OS-process payload. A worker process may
40	    carry multiple launch configs for colocated non-TP stages, while TP ranks
41	    each get their own launch config and process.
42	
43	    All string references (factory, merge_fn) are dotted import
44	    paths resolved by the child via :func:`import_string`.
45	    """
46	
47	    # Identity
48	    stage_name: str
49	    role: Literal["single", "leader", "follower"] = "single"
50	    tp_rank: int = 0
51	    tp_size: int = 1
52	    placement_gpu_id: int | None = None
53	    gpu_id: int | None = None
54	    nccl_port: int | None = None
55	
56	    # Factory
57	    factory: str = ""
58	    factory_args: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
59	    factory_arg_defaults: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
60	    env_defaults: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
61	
62	    # Routing: static next stage(s)
63	    next_stages: str | list[str] | None = None
64	    route_fn: str | None = None
65	    is_terminal: bool = False
66	
67	    # Fan-in
68	    wait_for: list[str] | None = None
69	    wait_for_fn: str | None = None
70	    merge_fn: str | None = None
71	    project_payload: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
72	
73	    # Communication pool/options. Transport selection belongs to CommRouter.
74	    comm_config: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
75	
76	    # Endpoints
77	    recv_endpoint: str = ""
78	    coordinator_endpoint: str = ""
79	    abort_endpoint: str = ""
80	    stage_endpoints: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
81	
82	    # Stream wiring
83	    stream_targets: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
84	    stream_done_to_fn: str | None = None
85	    # GPU-resident stage names (for the transport router to pick GPU vs host transport).
86	    gpu_stage_names: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
87	    stage_gpu_ids: dict[str, tuple[int, ...]] = field(default_factory=dict)
88	    # Explicit cross-node stage names. These edges use Mooncake when present.
89	    remote_stage_names: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
90	    is_stream_receiver: bool = False
91	    can_accept_stream_before_payload: bool = False
92	    disable_direct_cuda_ipc_payload: bool = False
93	
94	    # Same-process full payload wiring
95	    same_process_targets: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
96	
97	    # Fusion name map
98	    name_map: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
99	
100	    # TP internal control (leader -> followers)
101	    follower_work_queues: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)
102	    follower_abort_queues: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)
103	    follower_admin_result_queues: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)
104	    internal_work_queue: Any | None = None
105	    internal_abort_queue: Any | None = None
106	    internal_admin_result_queue: Any | None = None
107	
108	    @property
109	    def owns_external_io(self) -> bool:
110	        return self.role in {"single", "leader"}
111	
112	    @property
113	    def is_leader(self) -> bool:
114	        return self.role == "leader"
115	
116	    @property
117	    def is_follower(self) -> bool:
118	        return self.role == "follower"
119	
120	
121	@dataclass
122	class StageWorkerProcessSpec:
123	    """Everything one OS process needs to run one or more stages."""
124	
125	    process_name: str
126	    stage_specs: list[StageLaunchConfig]
127	
128	
129	def _get_worker_process_env(spec: StageWorkerProcessSpec) -> dict[str, str]:
130	    """Return the spawn-time env overrides for *spec*.
131	
132	    Hard invariant: a TP stage (``tp_size > 1``) must own its OS process
133	    exclusively. Its CUDA env remap and NCCL settings depend on being the sole
134	    tenant, so mixing a TP stage with any other stage in the same process group
135	    is a placement bug.
136	    """
137	    tp_stages = [s for s in spec.stage_specs if s.tp_size > 1]
138	    if not tp_stages:
139	        return {}
140	    if len(tp_stages) > 1 or len(spec.stage_specs) > 1:
141	        raise AssertionError(
142	            f"Process {spec.process_name!r} mixes a TP stage with other "
143	            "stages; TP stages must own their OS process exclusively. "
144	            f"stage_specs={[s.stage_name for s in spec.stage_specs]}"
145	        )
146	    return get_stage_process_env(tp_stages[0])
147	
148	
149	@contextmanager
150	def _patched_spawn_env(spec: StageWorkerProcessSpec):
151	    env_default_updates: dict[str, str] = {}
152	    for stage_spec in spec.stage_specs:
153	        for key, value in stage_spec.env_defaults.items():
154	            existing = env_default_updates.get(key)
155	            if existing is not None and existing != value:
156	                raise AssertionError(
157	                    f"Process {spec.process_name!r} has conflicting env default "
158	                    f"for {key!r}: {existing!r} != {value!r}"
159	                )
160	            if key not in os.environ:
161	                env_default_updates[key] = value
162	
163	    worker_process_env = _get_worker_process_env(spec)
164	    compat_env_defaults = get_gpu_compat_env_defaults(
165	        {
166	            **os.environ,
167	            **env_default_updates,
168	            **worker_process_env,
169	        }
170	    )
171	    updates = {
172	        **env_default_updates,
173	        **compat_env_defaults,
174	        **worker_process_env,
175	    }
176	    if not updates:
177	        yield
178	        return
179	
180	    backup = {key: os.environ.get(key) for key in updates}
181	    try:
182	        for key, value in updates.items():
183	            os.environ[key] = value
184	        yield
185	    finally:
186	        for key, value in backup.items():
187	            if value is None:
188	                os.environ.pop(key, None)
189	            else:
190	                os.environ[key] = value
191	
192	
193	class StageGroup:
194	    """Lifecycle manager for one or more OS processes in a topology group."""
195	
196	    def __init__(
197	        self,
198	        group_name: str,
199	        process_specs: Sequence[StageWorkerProcessSpec],
200	    ):
201	        if not process_specs:
202	            raise ValueError(
203	                f"StageGroup requires at least one process spec (group={group_name})"
204	            )
205	        self.group_name = group_name
206	        self.process_specs = list(process_specs)
207	        self._processes: list[multiprocessing.Process] = []
208	        self._ready_events: list[multiprocessing.Event] = []
209	        self._startup_error_channels: list[object] = []
210	
211	    @property
212	    def process_count(self) -> int:
213	        return len(self.process_specs)
214	
215	    @property
216	    def specs(self) -> list[StageLaunchConfig]:
217	        return [
218	            stage_spec
219	            for process_spec in self.process_specs
220	            for stage_spec in process_spec.stage_specs
221	        ]
222	
223	    @property
224	    def leader_spec(self) -> StageLaunchConfig:
225	        for spec in self.specs:
226	            if spec.role in {"single", "leader"}:
227	                return spec
228	        raise RuntimeError(f"StageGroup {self.group_name} has no leader-owned spec")
229	
230	    @property
231	    def leader_endpoint(self) -> str:
232	        """Control-plane recv endpoint for tp_rank 0 (used by Coordinator)."""
233	        return self.leader_spec.recv_endpoint
234	
235	    @property
236	    def stage_control_endpoints(self) -> dict[str, str]:
237	        return {
238	            spec.stage_name: spec.recv_endpoint
239	            for spec in self.specs
240	            if spec.owns_external_io
241	        }
242	
243	    @property
244	    def processes(self) -> list[multiprocessing.Process]:
245	        return list(self._processes)
246	
247	    def spawn(self, ctx: multiprocessing.context.SpawnContext) -> None:
248	        """Spawn the OS process(es) owned by this group."""
249	        for spec in self.process_specs:
250	            event = ctx.Event()
251	            startup_error_channel = ctx.Queue()
252	            proc_name = _process_name(spec)
253	            proc = ctx.Process(
254	                target=stage_process_main,
255	                args=(spec, event, startup_error_channel),
256	                name=proc_name,
257	                daemon=True,
258	            )
259	            try:
260	                with _patched_spawn_env(spec):
261	                    proc.start()
262	            except Exception:
263	                _close_queue(startup_error_channel)
264	                raise
265	            self._processes.append(proc)
266	            self._ready_events.append(event)
267	            self._startup_error_channels.append(startup_error_channel)
268	
269	        logger.info(
270	            "StageGroup %s: spawned %d process(es) (pids=%s)",
271	            self.group_name,
272	            len(self._processes),
273	            [p.pid for p in self._processes],
274	        )
275	
276	    async def wait_ready(self, timeout: float) -> None:
277	        """Block until every TP rank signals ready or *timeout* expires."""
278	        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
279	        deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout
280	
281	        for i, event in enumerate(self._ready_events):
282	            proc = self._processes[i]
283	            spec = self.process_specs[i]
284	            process_label = spec.process_name
285	            startup_error_channel = self._startup_error_channels[i]
286	
287	            while not event.is_set():
288	                remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()
289	                if remaining <= 0:
290	                    details = ""
291	                    try:
292	                        traceback_text = startup_error_channel.get_nowait()
293	                    except queue.Empty:
294	                        pass
295	                    else:
296	                        details = f"\nStartup failure detail:\n{traceback_text}"
297	                    raise TimeoutError(
298	                        f"Process {process_label} did not become ready "
299	                        f"within {timeout:.0f}s{details}"
300	                    )
301	                if not proc.is_alive():
302	                    details = ""
303	                    try:
304	                        traceback_text = startup_error_channel.get(timeout=0.2)
305	                    except queue.Empty:
306	                        pass
307	                    else:
308	                        details = f"\nStartup failure detail:\n{traceback_text}"
309	                    raise RuntimeError(
310	                        f"Process {process_label} died during startup "
311	                        f"(exit code {proc.exitcode}){details}"
312	                    )
313	                await loop.run_in_executor(None, event.wait, min(remaining, 1.0))
314	
315	            logger.info("Process %s ready", process_label)
316	
317	    def any_dead(self) -> bool:
318	        """Return True if any process in the group exited while runner is active."""
319	        return any(not p.is_alive() for p in self._processes)
320	
321	    def dead_summary(self) -> str:
322	        """Human-readable summary of dead processes (for error messages)."""
323	        parts = []
324	        for i, p in enumerate(self._processes):
325	            if not p.is_alive():
326	                process_spec = self.process_specs[i]
327	                parts.append(
328	                    f"{process_spec.process_name} " f"(pid={p.pid}, exit={p.exitcode})"
329	                )
330	        return ", ".join(parts) if parts else "(none)"
331	
332	    def close_control_channels(self) -> None:
333	        for q in self._startup_error_channels:
334	            _close_queue(q)
335	        for stage_spec in self.specs:
336	            for q in (
337	                stage_spec.follower_work_queues
338	                + stage_spec.follower_abort_queues
339	                + stage_spec.follower_admin_result_queues
340	            ):
341	                _close_queue(q)
342	
343	    async def shutdown(self, join_timeout: float = 30.0) -> None:
344	        try:
345	            for p in self._processes:
346	                p.join(timeout=join_timeout)
347	                if p.is_alive():
348	                    logger.warning(
349	                        "Terminating stuck process %s (pid=%s)",
350	                        p.name,
351	                        p.pid,
352	                    )
353	                    p.terminate()
354	                    p.join(timeout=5)
355	                    if p.is_alive():
356	                        p.kill()
357	                        p.join(timeout=2)
358	        finally:
359	            self.close_control_channels()
360	            self._processes.clear()
361	            self._ready_events.clear()
362	            self._startup_error_channels.clear()
363	
364	
365	def stage_process_main(
366	    spec: StageWorkerProcessSpec,
367	    ready_event: multiprocessing.Event,
368	    startup_error_channel: Any | None = None,
369	) -> None:
370	    """Subprocess entrypoint: construct stage(s) from *spec* and run them."""
371	    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, stream=sys.stdout)
372	    if not spec.stage_specs:
373	        raise ValueError(f"Process {spec.process_name!r} requires at least one stage")
374	    log = logging.getLogger(f"stage_workers.{spec.process_name}")
375	
376	    try:
377	        for stage_spec in spec.stage_specs:
378	            _prepare_cuda_environment(stage_spec, log)
379	        apply_gpu_compat_env_defaults()
380	        _run_process(spec, ready_event, log)
381	    except (KeyboardInterrupt, SystemExit):
382	        _destroy_torch_distributed_process_group(log)
383	        _reclaim_process_cuda_memory(
384	            _stage_gpu_ids(spec.stage_specs),
385	            log,
386	            reason=f"stage process {spec.process_name} terminated during startup",
387	        )
388	        raise
389	    except Exception as exc:
390	        import traceback
391	
392	        traceback_text = "".join(
393	            traceback.format_exception(type(exc), exc, exc.__traceback__)
394	        )
395	        with suppress(Exception):
396	            traceback.clear_frames(exc.__traceback__)
397	        log.error("Stage process %s failed\n%s", spec.process_name, traceback_text)
398	        _destroy_torch_distributed_process_group(log)
399	        _reclaim_process_cuda_memory(
400	            _stage_gpu_ids(spec.stage_specs),
401	            log,
402	            reason=f"stage process {spec.process_name} exit after failure",
403	        )
404	        if startup_error_channel is not None:
405	            startup_error_channel.put(traceback_text)
406	        sys.exit(1)
407	
408	
409	def _run_process(
410	    spec: StageWorkerProcessSpec,
411	    ready_event: multiprocessing.Event,
412	    log: logging.Logger,
413	) -> None:
414	    """Construct and drive all stages owned by one OS process.
415	
416	    Multi-stage semantics (since the topology PR):
417	    - All stages in ``spec.stage_specs`` share this OS process and one asyncio
418	      event loop. ``asyncio.gather`` runs them concurrently; **if any stage
419	      raises, the whole process exits** and ``MultiProcessPipelineRunner``'s
420	      ``_monitor_children`` will fail-all in-flight requests on the
421	      coordinator. There is no per-stage failure isolation inside one process
422	      group.
423	    - Scheduler construction is serialized by :func:`gpu_startup_lock` per GPU
424	      inside :func:`_construct_scheduler` — so when N stages on the same GPU
425	      live in this process, cold-start time degrades from ``max`` to ``sum``
426	      across them.
427	    """
428	    local_dispatcher = LocalStageDispatcher()
429	    stages: list[Stage] = []
430	
431	    async def _start_and_run():
432	        tasks: list[asyncio.Task] = []
433	        try:
434	            for stage in stages:
435	                await stage.start()
436	            log.info(
437	                "Process %s ready with stages=%s",
438	                spec.process_name,
439	                [stage.name for stage in stages],
440	            )
441	            ready_event.set()
442	            tasks = [asyncio.create_task(stage.run()) for stage in stages]
443	            await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
444	        finally:
445	            for task in tasks:
446	                task.cancel()
447	            if tasks:
448	                await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
449	            for stage in stages:
450	                if stage._running:
451	                    await stage.stop()
452	
453	    try:
454	        for stage_spec in spec.stage_specs:
455	            stages.append(
456	                _construct_stage(
457	                    stage_spec,
458	                    log,
459	                    local_dispatcher=local_dispatcher,
460	                )
461	            )
462	        local_dispatcher.register_many(stages)
463	        asyncio.run(_start_and_run())
464	    except BaseException:
465	        _cleanup_constructed_stages(
466	            stages,
467	            log,
468	            reason=f"stage process {spec.process_name} failure",
469	        )
470	        raise
471	
472	
473	def _cleanup_constructed_stages(
474	    stages: list[Stage],
475	    log: logging.Logger,
476	    *,
477	    reason: str,
478	) -> None:
479	    if stages:
480	        log.warning(
481	            "Cleaning up %d constructed stage(s) after %s",
482	            len(stages),
483	            reason,
484	        )
485	    for stage in reversed(stages):
486	        try:
487	            asyncio.run(stage.stop())
488	        except Exception as exc:
489	            log.warning(
490	                "Stage %s cleanup failed after process failure: %s",
491	                stage.name,
492	                exc,
493	                exc_info=True,
494	            )
495	        finally:
496	            stage.scheduler = None
497	
498	
499	def _stage_gpu_ids(stage_specs: Iterable[StageLaunchConfig]) -> list[int]:
500	    return sorted(
501	        {
502	            int(stage_spec.gpu_id)
503	            for stage_spec in stage_specs
504	            if stage_spec.gpu_id is not None
505	        }
506	    )
507	
508	
509	def _destroy_torch_distributed_process_group(log: logging.Logger) -> None:
510	    try:
511	        import torch.distributed as dist
512	
513	        if dist.is_available() and dist.is_initialized():
514	            log.warning("Destroying torch.distributed process group after failure")
515	            dist.destroy_process_group()
516	    except Exception as exc:
517	        log.warning(
518	            "torch.distributed cleanup failed after stage process failure: %s",
519	            exc,
520	            exc_info=True,
521	        )
522	
523	
524	def _reclaim_process_cuda_memory(
525	    gpu_ids: Iterable[int],
526	    log: logging.Logger,
527	    *,
528	    reason: str,
529	) -> None:
530	    gpu_id_list = list(gpu_ids)
531	    if not gpu_id_list:
532	        return
533	    gc.collect()
534	    try:
535	        import torch
536	
537	        if not torch.cuda.is_available():
538	            return
539	        log.warning(
540	            "Reclaiming CUDA memory after %s on gpu_ids=%s",
541	            reason,
542	            gpu_id_list,
543	        )
544	        for gpu_id in gpu_id_list:
545	            try:
546	                torch.cuda.set_device(int(gpu_id))
547	                with suppress(Exception):
548	                    torch.cuda.synchronize()
549	                torch.cuda.empty_cache()
550	                with suppress(Exception):
551	                    torch.cuda.ipc_collect()
552	            except Exception as exc:
553	                log.warning(
554	                    "CUDA memory reclaim failed for gpu_id=%s after %s: %s",
555	                    gpu_id,
556	                    reason,
557	                    exc,
558	                    exc_info=True,
559	                )
560	        gc.collect()
561	        log.warning(
562	            "CUDA memory reclaim complete after %s on gpu_ids=%s",
563	            reason,
564	            gpu_id_list,
565	        )
566	    except Exception as exc:
567	        log.warning(
568	            "CUDA memory reclaim skipped after %s: %s",
569	            reason,
570	            exc,
571	            exc_info=True,
572	        )
573	
574	
575	def _construct_stage(
576	    spec: StageLaunchConfig,
577	    log: logging.Logger,
578	    local_dispatcher: LocalStageDispatcher | None = None,
579	) -> Stage:
580	    gpu_id = spec.gpu_id
581	    if gpu_id is not None:
582	        import torch
583	
584	        torch.cuda.set_device(int(gpu_id))
585	        log.info("Set current CUDA device to %s for stage %s", gpu_id, spec.stage_name)
586	
587	    # --- Build scheduler via factory ---
588	    log.info(
589	        "Building scheduler for %s (tp_rank=%d/%d) ...",
590	        spec.stage_name,
591	        spec.tp_rank,
592	        spec.tp_size,
593	    )
594	
595	    scheduler = _construct_scheduler(spec, gpu_id, log)
596	
597	    def _target_list(targets: str | list[str] | None) -> list[str]:
598	        if targets is None:
599	            return []
600	        if isinstance(targets, str):
601	            return [targets]
602	        if isinstance(targets, list):
603	            return list(targets)
604	        raise ValueError(
605	            f"Dynamic route function for stage {spec.stage_name!r} returned "
606	            f"unsupported target value {targets!r}"
607	        )
608	
609	    def _map_target_list(targets: str | list[str] | None) -> list[str]:
610	        return [spec.name_map.get(t, t) for t in _target_list(targets)]
611	
612	    def _map_wait_source_list(sources: str | Iterable[str] | None) -> list[Any] | None:
613	        if sources is None:
614	            return None
615	        if isinstance(sources, str):
616	            return [spec.name_map.get(sources, sources)]
617	        if isinstance(sources, Iterable):
618	            return [
619	                spec.name_map.get(source, source) if isinstance(source, str) else source
620	                for source in sources
621	            ]
622	        raise ValueError(
623	            f"wait_for_fn for stage {spec.stage_name!r} returned unsupported "
624	            f"source value {sources!r}"
625	        )
626	
627	    def _target_result(
628	        targets: str | list[str] | None,
629	        *,
630	        allowed_targets: set[str],
631	        allow_empty: bool,
632	        hook_name: str,
633	    ) -> str | list[str] | None:
634	        mapped_targets = _map_target_list(targets)
635	        if not mapped_targets:
636	            if allow_empty:
637	                return None
638	            raise ValueError(
639	                f"{hook_name} for stage {spec.stage_name!r} returned no targets; "
640	                "dynamic route functions must return downstream stage(s)"
641	            )
642	        unknown = set(mapped_targets) - allowed_targets
643	        if unknown:
644	            raise ValueError(
645	                f"{hook_name} for stage {spec.stage_name!r} returned targets "
646	                f"outside the static topology: {sorted(unknown)}. "
647	                f"Allowed targets: {sorted(allowed_targets)}"
648	            )
649	        return mapped_targets[0] if isinstance(targets, str) else mapped_targets
650	
651	    # --- Build routing ---
652	    if spec.is_terminal:
653	        get_next = lambda request_id, output: None
654	    elif spec.route_fn:
655	        route_fn = import_string(spec.route_fn)
656	        allowed_route_targets = set(_map_target_list(spec.next_stages))
657	
658	        def get_next(request_id, output, _fn=route_fn):
659	            return _target_result(
660	                _fn(request_id, output),
661	                allowed_targets=allowed_route_targets,
662	                allow_empty=False,
663	                hook_name="route_fn",
664	            )
665	
666	    else:
667	        target = spec.next_stages
668	        if isinstance(target, str):
669	            mapped = spec.name_map.get(target, target)
670	            get_next = lambda request_id, output, _t=mapped: _t
671	        elif isinstance(target, list):
672	            mapped = [spec.name_map.get(t, t) for t in target]
673	            get_next = lambda request_id, output, _t=mapped: _t
674	        else:
675	            get_next = lambda request_id, output: None
676	
677	    if spec.stream_done_to_fn:
678	        stream_done_to_fn = import_string(spec.stream_done_to_fn)
679	        allowed_stream_targets = set(_map_target_list(spec.stream_targets))
680	        get_stream_done_targets = (
681	            lambda request_id, output, _fn=stream_done_to_fn: _target_result(
682	                _fn(request_id, output),
683	                allowed_targets=allowed_stream_targets,
684	                allow_empty=True,
685	                hook_name="stream_done_to_fn",
686	            )
687	        )
688	    else:
689	        get_stream_done_targets = None
690	
691	    # --- Build input handler ---
692	    if spec.wait_for and spec.merge_fn:
693	        merge_fn = import_string(spec.merge_fn)
694	        sources = {spec.name_map.get(n, n) for n in spec.wait_for}
695	        expected_sources_fn = None
696	        if spec.wait_for_fn:
697	            wait_for_fn = import_string(spec.wait_for_fn)
698	
699	            def expected_sources_fn(request_id, from_stage, data, _fn=wait_for_fn):
700	                resolved_sources = _fn(request_id, from_stage, data)
701	                return _map_wait_source_list(resolved_sources)
702	
703	        input_handler = AggregatedInput(
704	            sources=sources,
705	            merge=merge_fn,
706	            expected_sources_fn=expected_sources_fn,
707	        )
708	    else:
709	        input_handler = DirectInput()
710	    project_payload = {
711	        target: import_string(dotted_path)
712	        for target, dotted_path in spec.project_payload.items()
713	    }
714	
715	    if spec.owns_external_io:
716	        control_plane = StageControlPlane(
717	            stage_name=spec.stage_name,
718	            recv_endpoint=spec.recv_endpoint,
719	            coordinator_endpoint=spec.coordinator_endpoint,
720	            abort_endpoint=spec.abort_endpoint,
721	        )
722	    else:
723	        control_plane = TPFollowerControlPlane(
724	            stage_name=spec.stage_name,
725	            recv_endpoint=spec.recv_endpoint,
726	            work_queue=spec.internal_work_queue,
727	            abort_queue=spec.internal_abort_queue,
728	            admin_result_queue=spec.internal_admin_result_queue,
729	        )
730	
731	    tp_fanout = None
732	    if spec.is_leader:
733	        tp_fanout = TPLeaderFanout(
734	            stage_name=spec.stage_name,
735	            follower_work_queues=spec.follower_work_queues,
736	            follower_abort_queues=spec.follower_abort_queues,
737	            follower_admin_result_queues=spec.follower_admin_result_queues,
738	        )
739	
740	    # --- Construct Stage ---
741	    stage = Stage(
742	        name=spec.stage_name,
743	        role=spec.role,
744	        get_next=get_next,
745	        gpu_id=spec.gpu_id,
746	        placement_gpu_id=spec.placement_gpu_id,
747	        endpoints=spec.stage_endpoints,
748	        control_plane=control_plane,
749	        input_handler=input_handler,
750	        comm_config=spec.comm_config,
751	        scheduler=scheduler,
752	        project_payload=project_payload or None,
753	        stream_targets=spec.stream_targets or None,
754	        get_stream_done_targets=get_stream_done_targets,
755	        gpu_stage_names=spec.gpu_stage_names or None,
756	        stage_gpu_ids=spec.stage_gpu_ids or None,
757	        remote_stage_names=spec.remote_stage_names or None,
758	        same_process_targets=spec.same_process_targets or None,
759	        local_dispatcher=local_dispatcher,
760	        can_accept_stream_before_payload=spec.can_accept_stream_before_payload,
761	        disable_direct_cuda_ipc_payload=spec.disable_direct_cuda_ipc_payload,
762	        tp_fanout=tp_fanout,
763	        is_terminal=spec.is_terminal,
764	    )
765	
766	    if spec.is_stream_receiver:
767	        stage._stream_queue = StreamQueue(max_pending=4096)
768	
769	    return stage
770	
771	
772	def _construct_scheduler(
773	    spec: StageLaunchConfig,
774	    gpu_id: int | None,
775	    log: logging.Logger,
776	) -> Any:
777	    """Build a scheduler, serializing GPU factory work per visible device."""
778	
779	    factory = import_string(spec.factory)
780	    factory_args = resolve_factory_signature_args(
781	        factory,
782	        spec.factory_args,
783	        defaults=spec.factory_arg_defaults,
784	    )
785	    if gpu_id is None:
786	        return factory(**factory_args)
787	
788	    with gpu_startup_lock(int(gpu_id)) as lock_path:
789	        log.info(f"Acquired GPU startup lock for stage {spec.stage_name}: {lock_path}")
790	        return factory(**factory_args)
791	
792	
793	def get_stage_process_env(
794	    spec: StageLaunchConfig,
795	    env: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
796	) -> dict[str, str]:
797	    """Return per-process env overrides needed before TP child startup."""
798	    if spec.tp_size <= 1:
799	        return {}
800	
801	    source_env = env if env is not None else os.environ
802	    original_visible = source_env.get("CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES")
803	    if spec.gpu_id is None:
804	        raise ValueError(f"tp stage {spec.stage_name!r} requires a GPU id")
805	    if original_visible:
806	        visible_devices = [item.strip() for item in original_visible.split(",")]
807	        if spec.gpu_id >= len(visible_devices):
808	            raise ValueError(
809	                f"tp stage {spec.stage_name!r} assigned gpu_id={spec.gpu_id}, "
810	                f"but CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES only exposes {visible_devices}"
811	            )
812	        mapped_gpu = visible_devices[spec.gpu_id]
813	    else:
814	        mapped_gpu = str(spec.gpu_id)
815	
816	    return {
817	        "CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES": mapped_gpu,
818	        "SGLANG_ONE_VISIBLE_DEVICE_PER_PROCESS": "true",
819	        "SGLANG_ENABLE_TP_MEMORY_INBALANCE_CHECK": "false",
820	    }
821	
822	
823	def _prepare_cuda_environment(
824	    spec: StageLaunchConfig,
825	    log: logging.Logger,
826	) -> None:
827	    """Map TP rank processes to one visible CUDA device before torch init."""
828	    if os.environ.get("SGLANG_ONE_VISIBLE_DEVICE_PER_PROCESS") == "true":
829	        mapped_gpu = os.environ.get("CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES", str(spec.gpu_id))
830	        _normalize_spec_gpu_id_to_local_device(spec)
831	        log.info(
832	            "TP stage %s rank %d sees CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=%s (local gpu_id=0)",
833	            spec.stage_name,
834	            spec.tp_rank,
835	            mapped_gpu,
836	        )
837	        return
838	
839	    env_updates = get_stage_process_env(spec)
840	    if not env_updates:
841	        return
842	
843	    mapped_gpu = env_updates["CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES"]
844	    for key, value in env_updates.items():
845	        os.environ[key] = value
846	
847	    _normalize_spec_gpu_id_to_local_device(spec)
848	    log.info(
849	        "Mapped TP stage %s rank %d to CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=%s (local gpu_id=0)",
850	        spec.stage_name,
851	        spec.tp_rank,
852	        mapped_gpu,
853	    )
854	
855	
856	def _normalize_spec_gpu_id_to_local_device(spec: StageLaunchConfig) -> None:
857	    if spec.placement_gpu_id is None:
858	        spec.placement_gpu_id = spec.gpu_id
859	    spec.gpu_id = 0
860	    if "gpu_id" in spec.factory_arg_defaults:
861	        spec.factory_arg_defaults["gpu_id"] = 0
862	    if "gpu_id" in spec.comm_config:
863	        spec.comm_config["gpu_id"] = 0
864	
865	
866	def _process_name(spec: StageWorkerProcessSpec) -> str:
867	    if len(spec.stage_specs) > 1:
868	        return f"process-{spec.process_name}"
869	    stage_spec = spec.stage_specs[0]
870	    if stage_spec.role == "single":
871	        return f"stage-{stage_spec.stage_name}"
872	    if stage_spec.role == "leader":
873	        return f"stage-{stage_spec.stage_name}-leader"
874	    return f"stage-{stage_spec.stage_name}-tp{stage_spec.tp_rank}-follower"
875	
876	
877	def _close_queue(q: object) -> None:
878	    q.close()
879	    join_thread = getattr(q, "join_thread", None)
880	    if callable(join_thread):
881	        join_thread()
882

content.content:
1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	"""Stage placement planning and validation for Omni pipelines."""
3	
4	from __future__ import annotations
5	
6	import inspect
7	from collections import defaultdict
8	from dataclasses import dataclass
9	from typing import Protocol
10	
11	from sglang_omni.config.runtime import reject_untyped_total_gpu_memory_fraction
12	from sglang_omni.config.schema import PipelineConfig, StageConfig
13	from sglang_omni.utils.imports import import_string
14	
15	
16	@dataclass(frozen=True)
17	class StagePlacement:
18	    stage_name: str
19	    gpu_ids: tuple[int, ...]
20	    tp_size: int
21	    total_gpu_memory_fraction: float | None
22	
23	
24	@dataclass(frozen=True)
25	class GpuPlacement:
26	    gpu_id: int
27	    stage_names: tuple[str, ...]
28	    total_gpu_memory_fraction: float
29	    has_memory_fraction: bool
30	    missing_fraction_stage_names: tuple[str, ...]
31	
32	
33	@dataclass(frozen=True)
34	class StagePlacementPlan:
35	    stages: dict[str, StagePlacement]
36	    gpus: dict[int, GpuPlacement]
37	
38	
39	class PlacementPolicy(Protocol):
40	    def validate(self, config: PipelineConfig, plan: StagePlacementPlan) -> None: ...
41	
42	
43	class StagePlacementPlanner:
44	    """Build a model-agnostic placement plan from pipeline stage config."""
45	
46	    def __init__(self, config: PipelineConfig):
47	        self._config = config
48	
49	    def build(
50	        self,
51	        *,
52	        stages_cfg: list[StageConfig] | None = None,
53	        apply_policy: bool = True,
54	    ) -> StagePlacementPlan:
55	        stages = stages_cfg if stages_cfg is not None else self._config.stages
56	        placements: dict[str, StagePlacement] = {}
57	        gpu_entries: dict[int, list[tuple[str, float | None]]] = defaultdict(list)
58	
59	        for stage in stages:
60	            reject_untyped_total_gpu_memory_fraction(
61	                stage.name,
62	                stage.factory_args,
63	                self._config.runtime_overrides.get(stage.name, {}),
64	            )
65	            gpu_ids = _resolve_stage_gpu_ids(stage)
66	            if not gpu_ids:
67	                continue
68	
69	            fraction = stage.runtime.resources.total_gpu_memory_fraction
70	            placements[stage.name] = StagePlacement(
71	                stage_name=stage.name,
72	                gpu_ids=gpu_ids,
73	                tp_size=stage.tp_size,
74	                total_gpu_memory_fraction=fraction,
75	            )
76	            for gpu_id in gpu_ids:
77	                gpu_entries[gpu_id].append((stage.name, fraction))
78	
79	        gpu_plans = {
80	            gpu_id: _build_gpu_placement(gpu_id, entries)
81	            for gpu_id, entries in gpu_entries.items()
82	        }
83	        plan = StagePlacementPlan(
84	            stages=placements,
85	            gpus=gpu_plans,
86	        )
87	        self._validate_memory_budgets(plan)
88	        if apply_policy:
89	            _apply_placement_policy(self._config, plan)
90	        return plan
91	
92	    def _validate_memory_budgets(self, plan: StagePlacementPlan) -> None:
93	        limit = self._config.placement.max_total_gpu_memory_fraction_per_gpu
94	        for gpu in plan.gpus.values():
95	            if gpu.total_gpu_memory_fraction > limit + 1e-9:
96	                raise ValueError(
97	                    f"GPU {gpu.gpu_id} total_gpu_memory_fraction="
98	                    f"{gpu.total_gpu_memory_fraction:.3f} exceeds placement limit "
99	                    f"{limit:.3f}"
100	                )
101	
102	
103	def build_stage_placement_plan(
104	    config: PipelineConfig,
105	    *,
106	    stages_cfg: list[StageConfig] | None = None,
107	    apply_policy: bool = True,
108	) -> StagePlacementPlan:
109	    return StagePlacementPlanner(config).build(
110	        stages_cfg=stages_cfg,
111	        apply_policy=apply_policy,
112	    )
113	
114	
115	def resolve_stage_gpu_ids(
116	    plan: StagePlacementPlan,
117	    stage_cfg: StageConfig,
118	) -> list[int | None]:
119	    placement = plan.stages.get(stage_cfg.name)
120	    if placement is None:
121	        return [None] * stage_cfg.tp_size
122	    return list(placement.gpu_ids)
123	
124	
125	def resolve_gpu_stage_names(plan: StagePlacementPlan) -> set[str]:
126	    """Names of all GPU-resident stages.
127	
128	    The placement planner only records stages that resolve to a GPU (CPU-only
129	    stages are skipped), so the plan's stage keys are exactly the GPU stages.
130	    The transport router uses this to decide CUDA-IPC vs SHM per edge.
131	    """
132	    return set(plan.stages.keys())
133	
134	
135	def _resolve_stage_gpu_ids(stage: StageConfig) -> tuple[int, ...]:
136	    gpu = stage.gpu
137	    if gpu is None:
138	        return ()
139	    if isinstance(gpu, int):
140	        if stage.tp_size > 1:
141	            raise ValueError(
142	                f"Stage {stage.name!r}: TP placement requires a list of "
143	                f"{stage.tp_size} unique GPU ids, got scalar gpu={gpu}"
144	            )
145	        return tuple(gpu for _ in range(stage.tp_size))
146	    if len(gpu) != stage.tp_size:
147	        raise ValueError(
148	            f"Stage {stage.name!r}: gpu has {len(gpu)} entries "
149	            f"but tp_size={stage.tp_size}"
150	        )
151	    gpu_ids = tuple(int(gpu_id) for gpu_id in gpu)
152	    if len(set(gpu_ids)) != len(gpu_ids):
153	        raise ValueError(
154	            f"Stage {stage.name!r}: TP placement requires unique GPU ids, "
155	            f"got {list(gpu_ids)}"
156	        )
157	    return gpu_ids
158	
159	
160	def _build_gpu_placement(
161	    gpu_id: int,
162	    entries: list[tuple[str, float | None]],
163	) -> GpuPlacement:
164	    total = 0.0
165	    has_memory_fraction = False
166	    missing: set[str] = set()
167	    stage_names: list[str] = []
168	    for stage_name, fraction in entries:
169	        stage_names.append(stage_name)
170	        if fraction is None:
171	            missing.add(stage_name)
172	            continue
173	        has_memory_fraction = True
174	        total += fraction
175	    return GpuPlacement(
176	        gpu_id=gpu_id,
177	        stage_names=tuple(stage_names),
178	        total_gpu_memory_fraction=total,
179	        has_memory_fraction=has_memory_fraction,
180	        missing_fraction_stage_names=tuple(sorted(missing)),
181	    )
182	
183	
184	def _apply_placement_policy(
185	    config: PipelineConfig,
186	    plan: StagePlacementPlan,
187	) -> None:
188	    if config.placement_policy is None:
189	        return
190	    policy = import_string(config.placement_policy)
191	    if inspect.isclass(policy):
192	        policy = policy()
193	    if hasattr(policy, "validate"):
194	        policy.validate(config, plan)
195	        return
196	    if callable(policy):
197	        policy(config, plan)
198	        return
199	    raise TypeError(
200	        f"placement_policy {config.placement_policy!r} must be callable or expose "
201	        "validate(config, plan)"
202	    )
203

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	"""Runtime preparation helpers shared by pipeline runners."""
3	
4	from __future__ import annotations
5	
6	import logging
7	import re
8	import shutil
9	import tempfile
10	from dataclasses import dataclass
11	from pathlib import Path
12	
13	import zmq
14	
15	from sglang_omni.config.placement import StagePlacementPlan, build_stage_placement_plan
16	from sglang_omni.config.schema import PipelineConfig, StageConfig
17	from sglang_omni.config.topology import ProcessTopologyPlan, build_process_topology_plan
18	
19	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
20	
21	# PyZMQ checks the filesystem path after ``ipc://`` against this budget.
22	_IPC_SUN_PATH_BUDGET = getattr(zmq, "IPC_PATH_MAX_LEN", 100)
23	_TEMPFILE_RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN = 8
24	
25	
26	class IpcRuntimeDir:
27	    """Runtime-owned IPC directory for one pipeline instance."""
28	
29	    def __init__(self, path: Path):
30	        self.path = path
31	        self._closed = False
32	
33	    def __enter__(self) -> IpcRuntimeDir:
34	        return self
35	
36	    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb) -> None:
37	        self.close()
38	
39	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
40	        return f"IpcRuntimeDir(path={self.path!r}, closed={self._closed})"
41	
42	    def close(self) -> None:
43	        if self._closed:
44	            return
45	        self._closed = True
46	        try:
47	            shutil.rmtree(self.path)
48	        except FileNotFoundError:
49	            return
50	        except OSError as exc:
51	            logger.warning("Failed to remove IPC runtime dir %s: %s", self.path, exc)
52	
53	
54	@dataclass(frozen=True)
55	class PipelineRuntimePrep:
56	    """Prepared stage, endpoint, placement, and topology state."""
57	
58	    stages_cfg: list[StageConfig]
59	    name_map: dict[str, str]
60	    entry_stage: str
61	    endpoints: dict[str, str]
62	    placement_plan: StagePlacementPlan
63	    process_plan: ProcessTopologyPlan
64	    runtime_dir: IpcRuntimeDir
65	    runtime_dir_created_here: bool
66	
67	
68	def create_ipc_runtime_dir(
69	    config: PipelineConfig,
70	    *,
71	    stages: list[StageConfig] | None = None,
72	) -> IpcRuntimeDir:
73	    """Create a per-run IPC namespace for one pipeline instance."""
74	    base_root = Path(config.endpoints.base_path)
75	    base_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
76	    if stages is None:
77	        stages, _, _ = config.apply_fusion()
78	
79	    namespace_prefix = re.sub(r"[^0-9a-z]+", "-", config.name.lower()).strip("-")

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
"""Runtime preparation helpers shared by pipeline runners."""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import re
import shutil
import tempfile
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path

import zmq

from sglang_omni.config.placement import StagePlacementPlan, build_stage_placement_plan
from sglang_omni.config.schema import PipelineConfig, StageConfig
from sglang_omni.config.topology import ProcessTopologyPlan, build_process_topology_plan

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# PyZMQ checks the filesystem path after ``ipc://`` against this budget.
_IPC_SUN_PATH_BUDGET = getattr(zmq, "IPC_PATH_MAX_LEN", 100)
_TEMPFILE_RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN = 8


class IpcRuntimeDir:
    """Runtime-owned IPC directory for one pipeline instance."""

    def __init__(self, path: Path):
        self.path = path
        self._closed = False

    def __enter__(self) -> IpcRuntimeDir:
        return self

    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb) -> None:
        self.close()

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        return f"IpcRuntimeDir(path={self.path!r}, closed={self._closed})"

    def close(self) -> None:
        if self._closed:
            return
        self._closed = True
        try:
            shutil.rmtree(self.path)
        except FileNotFoundError:
            return
        except OSError as exc:
            logger.warning("Failed to remove IPC runtime dir %s: %s", self.path, exc)


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PipelineRuntimePrep:
    """Prepared stage, endpoint, placement, and topology state."""

    stages_cfg: list[StageConfig]
    name_map: dict[str, str]
    entry_stage: str
    endpoints: dict[str, str]
    placement_plan: StagePlacementPlan
    process_plan: ProcessTopologyPlan
    runtime_dir: IpcRuntimeDir
    runtime_dir_created_here: bool


def create_ipc_runtime_dir(
    config: PipelineConfig,
    *,
    stages: list[StageConfig] | None = None,
) -> IpcRuntimeDir:
    """Create a per-run IPC namespace for one pipeline instance."""
    base_root = Path(config.endpoints.base_path)
    base_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    if stages is None:
        stages, _, _ = config.apply_fusion()

    namespace_prefix = re.sub(r"[^0-9a-z]+", "-", config.name.lower()).strip("-")
    if not namespace_prefix:
        namespace_prefix = "pipeline"
    namespace_prefix = _truncate_ipc_namespace_prefix(
        namespace_prefix,
        base_root=base_root,
        stages=stages,
    )
    dir_prefix = f"{namespace_prefix}-" if namespace_prefix else ""
    path = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix=dir_prefix, dir=base_root))
    return IpcRuntimeDir(path)


def prepare_pipeline_runtime(
    config: PipelineConfig,
    *,
    ipc_runtime_dir: IpcRuntimeDir | None = None,
) -> PipelineRuntimePrep:
    """Prepare fused stages, endpoint allocation, and process topology."""
    stages_cfg, name_map, entry_stage = config.apply_fusion()
    runtime_dir = ipc_runtime_dir
    if runtime_dir is None:
        runtime_dir = create_ipc_runtime_dir(config, stages=stages_cfg)
        runtime_dir_created_here = True
    else:
        runtime_dir_created_here = False

    try:
        placement_plan = build_stage_placement_plan(config, stages_cfg=stages_cfg)
        process_plan = build_process_topology_plan(
            config,
            placement_plan,
            stages_cfg=stages_cfg,
        )
        endpoints = allocate_endpoints(
            stages=stages_cfg,
            ipc_base_dir=runtime_dir.path,
        )
    except Exception:
        if runtime_dir_created_here:
            runtime_dir.close()
        raise

    return PipelineRuntimePrep(
        stages_cfg=stages_cfg,
        name_map=name_map,
        entry_stage=entry_stage,
        endpoints=endpoints,
        placement_plan=placement_plan,
        process_plan=process_plan,
        runtime_dir=runtime_dir,
        runtime_dir_created_here=runtime_dir_created_here,
    )


def build_comm_config(
    stage_cfg: StageConfig,
) -> dict:
    comm_cfg = stage_cfg.comm
    if comm_cfg is not None:
        return {
            "slot_size_mb": comm_cfg.slot_size_mb,
            "credits": comm_cfg.credits,
            "cuda_ipc_slot_size_kb": comm_cfg.cuda_ipc_slot_size_kb,
            "cuda_ipc_pool_size_mb": comm_cfg.cuda_ipc_pool_size_mb,
            "mooncake_protocol": comm_cfg.mooncake_protocol,
            "mooncake_hostname": comm_cfg.mooncake_hostname,
            "mooncake_device_name": comm_cfg.mooncake_device_name,
        }

    return {
        "slot_size_mb": 512,
        "credits": 2,
        "cuda_ipc_slot_size_kb": 64,
        "cuda_ipc_pool_size_mb": None,
        "mooncake_protocol": "rdma",
        "mooncake_hostname": None,
        "mooncake_device_name": "",
    }


def allocate_endpoints(
    *,
    stages: list[StageConfig],
    ipc_base_dir: Path,
) -> dict[str, str]:
    _validate_ipc_endpoint_budget(stages=stages, ipc_base_dir=ipc_base_dir)
    base_dir = ipc_base_dir
    endpoints = {
        "completion": f"ipc://{base_dir}/completion.sock",
        "abort": f"ipc://{base_dir}/abort.sock",
    }
    for stage in stages:
        endpoints[f"stage_{stage.name}"] = f"ipc://{base_dir}/stage_{stage.name}.sock"
    return endpoints


def _truncate_ipc_namespace_prefix(
    namespace_prefix: str,
    *,
    base_root: Path,
    stages: list[StageConfig],
) -> str:
    endpoint_suffix_len = _longest_endpoint_suffix_len(stages)
    min_dir_len = _ipc_dir_len(
        base_root=base_root,
        namespace_prefix_len=0,
        endpoint_suffix_len=endpoint_suffix_len,
    )
    if min_dir_len > _IPC_SUN_PATH_BUDGET:
        _raise_ipc_path_budget_error(
            base_path=base_root,
            endpoint_suffix_len=endpoint_suffix_len,
            path_len=min_dir_len,
        )

    max_prefix_len = (
        _IPC_SUN_PATH_BUDGET
        - len(str(base_root))
        - len("/")
        - len("-")
        - _TEMPFILE_RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN
        - endpoint_suffix_len
    )
    if max_prefix_len <= 0:
        return ""
    return namespace_prefix[:max_prefix_len]


def _validate_ipc_endpoint_budget(
    *,
    stages: list[StageConfig],
    ipc_base_dir: Path,
) -> None:
    endpoint_suffix_len = _longest_endpoint_suffix_len(stages)
    path_len = len(str(ipc_base_dir)) + endpoint_suffix_len
    if path_len > _IPC_SUN_PATH_BUDGET:
        _raise_ipc_path_budget_error(
            base_path=ipc_base_dir,
            endpoint_suffix_len=endpoint_suffix_len,
            path_len=path_len,
        )


def _longest_endpoint_suffix_len(stages: list[StageConfig]) -> int:
    suffixes = [len("/completion.sock"), len("/abort.sock")]
    suffixes.extend(len(f"/stage_{stage.name}.sock") for stage in stages)
    return max(suffixes)


def _ipc_dir_len(
    *,
    base_root: Path,
    namespace_prefix_len: int,
    endpoint_suffix_len: int,
) -> int:
    dir_name_len = namespace_prefix_len + _TEMPFILE_RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN
    if namespace_prefix_len:
        dir_name_len += len("-")
    return len(str(base_root)) + len("/") + dir_name_len + endpoint_suffix_len


def _raise_ipc_path_budget_error(
    *,
    base_path: Path,
    endpoint_suffix_len: int,
    path_len: int,
) -> None:
    raise ValueError(
        "IPC endpoint path would exceed the Unix-domain socket path limit "
        f"({_IPC_SUN_PATH_BUDGET} chars): base path {str(base_path)!r} plus "
        f"longest endpoint suffix length {endpoint_suffix_len} yields {path_len} "
        "chars. Shorten endpoints.base_path or stage names."
    )

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	"""Pipeline configuration for Qwen3-Omni."""
3	
4	from __future__ import annotations
5	
6	from typing import ClassVar

12	_PKG = "sglang_omni.models.qwen3_omni"

17	# SGLang reads this when DeepGEMM compile utilities are imported. Qwen AR
18	# stages can first hit some dense FP8 shapes after readiness; disable all-M
19	# precompile so that miss does not become a long post-ready compile session.
20	# FIXME (Ratish): Replace this with a bounded/pre-ready SGLang DeepGEMM compile
21	# policy once that exists outside import-time environment globals.
22	_DEEPGEMM_PRECOMPILE_ENV_DEFAULTS = {"SGLANG_JIT_DEEPGEMM_PRECOMPILE": "0"}
23	
24	# A colocated worker launches eight stage processes. Letting every PyTorch
25	# process size its OpenMP pool to the full host oversubscribes launch-side CPU
26	# work when multiple workers share a node. Preprocessing handles one prompt per
27	# scheduler call, so a host-wide tokenizer Rayon pool only adds contention.
28	_COLOCATED_STAGE_ENV_DEFAULTS = {
29	    **_DEEPGEMM_PRECOMPILE_ENV_DEFAULTS,
30	    "OMP_NUM_THREADS": "8",
31	    "TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM": "false",
32	}
33	
34	
35	def _preprocessing_stage(*, process: str) -> StageConfig:
36	    return StageConfig(
37	        name="preprocessing",
38	        process=process,
39	        factory=f"{_PKG}.stages.create_preprocessing_executor",
40	        factory_args={"thinker_max_seq_len": 8192},
41	        runtime_arg_map={

47	        project_payload={
48	            "image_encoder": (
49	                f"{_PKG}.request_builders.project_preprocessing_to_image_encoder"
50	            ),
51	            "audio_encoder": (
52	                f"{_PKG}.request_builders.project_preprocessing_to_audio_encoder"
53	            ),
54	            "mm_aggregate": (

56	            ),
57	        },
58	    )

71	        },

85	        },

90	    *, process: str, gpu: int, speech_enabled: bool = False
91	) -> StageConfig:
92	    # Route the merged payload to talker_ar so partial-start can fire — the
93	    # policy hook needs the new_request before `stream_done` arrives.

99	            gpu=gpu,
100	            wait_for=["preprocessing", "image_encoder", "audio_encoder"],

102	            merge_fn=f"{_PKG}.merge.merge_for_thinker",

109	                ),
110	            },
111	        )
112	    return StageConfig(
113	        name="mm_aggregate",
114	        process=process,
115	        factory=f"{_PKG}.stages.create_aggregate_executor",
116	        gpu=gpu,
117	        wait_for=["preprocessing", "image_encoder", "audio_encoder"],

127	class StageConfig(BaseModel):
128	    """Single pipeline stage configuration.
129

132	        StageConfig(name="decode", factory="...create_decode", terminal=True)
133	
134	    Fan-in example::

188	    def model_post_init(self, __context: Any = None) -> None:
189	        fields_set = self.__pydantic_fields_set__
190	        tp_size_set = "tp_size" in fields_set
191	        parallelism_set = "parallelism" in fields_set
192	        if self.tp_size < 1:
193	            raise ValueError(f"Stage {self.name!r} must have tp_size >= 1")
194	        if self.process is not None:
195	            self.process = self.process.strip()
196	            if not self.process:

198	        if parallelism_set and tp_size_set and self.parallelism.tp != self.tp_size:
199	            raise ValueError(

201	                f"parallelism.tp={self.parallelism.tp}"
202	            )
203	        if not parallelism_set and self.tp_size != self.parallelism.tp:
204	            self.parallelism.tp = self.tp_size
205	        elif (

207	        ):

380	                        f"Stage {s.name!r} next has unknown stages: {sorted(unknown)}"
381	                    )
382	            for t in s.stream_to:
383	                if t not in names:
384	                    raise ValueError(
385	                        f"Stage {s.name!r} stream_to references unknown stage {t!r}"
386	                    )
387	            for t in s.project_payload:
388	                if t not in names:
389	                    raise ValueError(
390	                        f"Stage {s.name!r} project_payload references unknown stage {t!r}"
391	                    )
392	
393	        for stage_name in self.runtime_overrides:
394	            if stage_name not in names:
395	                raise ValueError(
396	                    f"runtime_overrides references unknown stage {stage_name!r}"
397	                )
398	
399	        missing_process = [
400	            s.name for s in self.stages if s.tp_size == 1 and not s.process
401	        ]
402	        if missing_process:
403	            raise ValueError(
404	                "Non-TP stages must declare process; "
405	                f"missing process for {missing_process}"
406	            )
407	
408	    def _validate_fusion(self) -> None:
409	        names = [s.name for s in self.stages]
410	        fused = self.fused_stages or []
411	        if not fused:
412	            return
413	        index_map = {n: i for i, n in enumerate(names)}
414	        stage_by_name = {s.name: s for s in self.stages}
415	        seen: set[str] = set()
416	        for group in fused:
417	            if not group or len(group) < 2:
418	                raise ValueError("fused_stages groups must have at least 2 stage names")
419	            for n in group:
420	                if n not in index_map:
421	                    raise ValueError(f"fused stage {n!r} is not defined")
422	                if n in seen:
423	                    raise ValueError(f"stage {n!r} appears in multiple fused groups")
424	                seen.add(n)
425	            indices = [index_map[n] for n in group]
426	            if indices != list(range(indices[0], indices[0] + len(indices))):
427	                raise ValueError(f"fused group not adjacent/ordered: {group}")
428	            self._validate_fused_group_contract(group, stage_by_name)
429	
430	    def _validate_fused_group_contract(
431	        self,
432	        group: list[str],
433	        stage_by_name: dict[str, StageConfig],
434	    ) -> None:
435	        stages = [stage_by_name[name] for name in group]
436	
437	        for stage in stages:
438	            if stage.tp_size != 1:
439	                raise ValueError(
440	                    f"fused group {group} cannot include TP stage {stage.name!r}"
441	                )
442	
443	        gpu_ids = {
444	            gpu_id for stage in stages for gpu_id in _stage_gpu_ids_for_fusion(stage)
445	        }
446	        if len(gpu_ids) > 1:
447	            raise ValueError(
448	                f"fused group {group} must fit on one GPU; got {sorted(gpu_ids)}"
449	            )
450	
451	        for index, stage in enumerate(stages):
452	            if index > 0 and stage.wait_for:
453	                raise ValueError(
454	                    f"fused group {group} cannot include internal fan-in stage "
455	                    f"{stage.name!r}"
456	                )
457	            if index < len(stages) - 1:
458	                expected_next = group[index + 1]
459	                if stage.terminal or stage.route_fn is not None:
460	                    raise ValueError(
461	                        f"fused group {group} must be linear; internal stage "
462	                        f"{stage.name!r} cannot be terminal or dynamic-routed"
463	                    )
464	                if _target_list(stage.next) != [expected_next]:
465	                    raise ValueError(
466	                        f"fused group {group} must be linear; stage "
467	                        f"{stage.name!r} must route only to {expected_next!r}"
468	                    )
469	
470	    def apply_fusion(self) -> tuple[list[StageConfig], dict[str, str], str]:
471	        name_map = {s.name: s.name for s in self.stages}
472	        return list(self.stages), name_map, self.resolved_entry_stage
473	
474	    @staticmethod
475	    def from_dict(data: dict[str, Any]) -> PipelineConfig:
476	        return PipelineConfig(**data)
477	
478	
479	def _target_list(targets: str | list[str] | None) -> list[str]:
480	    if targets is None:
481	        return []
482	    if isinstance(targets, str):
483	        return [targets]
484	    return list(targets)
485	
486	
487	def _stage_gpu_ids_for_fusion(stage: StageConfig) -> tuple[int, ...]:
488	    gpu = stage.gpu
489	    if gpu is None:
490	        return ()
491	    if isinstance(gpu, int):
492	        return (gpu,)
493	    return tuple(int(gpu_id) for gpu_id in gpu)
494
